expectations.text: Removes 'Think through' prompts, scaffold elements, status markers, and Raw Material sections
expectations.evidence: No 'Think through' prompts, no '> Status:' markers, no 'Raw Material' section, no scaffold artifacts. Clean article throughout.

expectations.text: Title is the sharpest or most memorable line from the lesson, not a generic description
expectations.evidence: Title: 'A Working Demo Doesn't Let You Be Vague' — the sharpest line from the lesson, used as previously established in the lesson 01 publishing process.

expectations.text: Output is clean prose suitable for Substack — no bullet-only sections, no scaffold artifacts
expectations.evidence: All sections are clean prose with proper headers. No bullet-only sections. Subtitle, section breaks, and bio line all formatted for Substack.

expectations.text: Maintains the author's honest, direct tone — including uncertainty and 'what I'm still figuring out'
expectations.evidence: 'What I'm still figuring out' section preserved with honest admissions: 'I didn't save time — I compressed decisions', 'The old safety net of slow iteration is gone. I don't have a replacement yet', 'the honest answer is both'.

expectations.text: Includes a setup paragraph framing the problem for readers who don't know the context
expectations.evidence: Opening paragraph: 'Here's how product development used to work at my company...' — establishes the broken workflow and stakes before introducing the solution.

expectations.text: Includes a one-line bio at the end
expectations.evidence: 'Derek is a PM based in Hong Kong. This is the first in a series about what he's learning building with AI.'

expectations.text: Does not invent examples, anecdotes, or claims not present in the source lesson
expectations.evidence: All examples (Pokemon API, merchant pinning, 13 Golden Conversations, COO feedback) and claims trace directly to the lesson file. No invented content.
